artifact: Architecture decision: Next.js 14 + TypeScript + Tailwind + commerce-core + verbatim UI port

evidence: Option A (selected): Next.js 14 + TypeScript + Tailwind, packages/commerce-core, verbatim UI port from docs/atm-415/ui/onereel
Option B (rejected): SvelteKit — outside the proven ENVELOPE.md (4 sealed Next.js apps; Svelte has zero sealed examples)
Option C (rejected): Static HTML + vanilla JS — cannot support App Router dynamic params, server components, or the existing UI source
Option D (rejected): Custom commerce engine from scratch — commerce-core already built and 58/58 tests pass; reinvention wastes budget
alternatives_compared.notes: Four options considered. Option A is the only one within the proven envelope with a pre-built engine and provided UI.

evidence: Reversibility: HIGH — the app is local-only, no deployed dependencies. Pivot means wiping apps/video-storefront/ and starting over with a different stack.
Rollback cost: low — no production data, no real users, no deployed infrastructure
ENGINEERING_REQUIRED would surface if the stack cannot support a needed UI pattern
reversibility_labeled.notes: Fully reversible. Local-only app with no external dependencies.

evidence: Intent: patron-facing single-film storefront with pay-per-film + maker credit + instant digital access
Next.js App Router: handles film/[id], checkout/[id], backed/[id] dynamic routes natively
commerce-core: provides cart/checkout/order/events — exact fit for the pay-per-film flow
Tailwind: UI tokens already defined in docs/atm-415/ui/onereel/styles/tokens.css
fit_to_intent.notes: Perfect fit: the stack was used to design the provided UI. No impedance mismatch.

evidence: All four options were evaluated against the ENVELOPE.md proven record
Selection wasn't made without considering alternatives
The rejection of SvelteKit/vanilla/custom-engine is evidence-based, not preference-based
option_fairness.notes: Options evaluated fairly. The envelope record is the objective criterion.

evidence: Tradeoff accepted: Next.js adds SSR complexity vs a simpler SPA, but SSR is required for film/[id] server components
Tradeoff accepted: verbatim UI port constrains visual creativity but eliminates design risk and closes the 3.6/10 gap
Tradeoff accepted: commerce-core is synthetic-only — no real payment capability, but that's the intent
tradeoff_clarity.notes: Tradeoffs are explicit. The verbatim-port constraint is a deliberate choice to eliminate the prior 3.6/10 design-quality gap.

evidence: No custom auth — DemoUserChip + localStorage (spec: 'no real auth')
No database — lib/films.ts is a static array; wired to commerce-core in memory
No payment provider — synthetic PaymentForm (spec: synthetic only)
No search/filter (spec: out of scope by design)
No dark mode (brand: light mode only)
anti_overbuild.notes: Selection explicitly excludes every 'nice to have' that's out of scope. Minimal surface.

overall_notes: Selection is straightforward: the provided UI, proven stack, and pre-built engine make this a port not a build decision. No structural change needed; no ENGINEERING_REQUIRED stop. Fully reversible.
